description: Letter from Yosef b. Shemuel al-Dani in Palermo to Isma’il b. Avraham in Damsis. The first part of the letter deals with the tragedy that happened in the sea on the way to Sicily. A ship sank between Surt (Sirte) and Hawwāra in modern-day Libya. Hawwāra is not Lebda, as suggested by Goitein. In the 13th century, the port of Hawwāra was known as Qaṣr Aḥmad (Ibn Rushayd al-Sabtī, Riḥla, 6: 5), which became known as Misrata in the 16th century (AP). After the writer arrived in Sicily, he found out that he had been expelled from his house. In the other part, Yosef writes about his wife that is still in Egypt. He wrote her a divorce certificate in case he will not be able to come back. He is willing to sell his land and take the risk to come back to Egypt, to take her and their son to Palermo, if she swears she will go with him. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 2, #173) VMR
description_he: מכתב מיוסף בן שמואל אל-דאני בפלרמו לישמעאל בן אברהם בדמסיס, על אסון ספינה בלוב ועל אשתו שנותרה במצרים
